notes: One source (Bajaj Finance) separately quotes 31.12 km/kg for the same CNG variant; used the figure repeated across the most sources (33.73).

Aggregator figure for the 1.0L K10C engine; Maruti's own site only publishes 1.2L-specific figures (see the two 1.2L rows below), so this MT/AMT pair is cross-checked across ZigWheels, CarDekho and 91Wheels but not manufacturer-confirmed directly.
Maruti's own site states a rounder ~33.47 km/kg for the same CNG variant; ZigWheels/CarDekho converge on 34.05. Flagging the ~0.6 km/kg disagreement between manufacturer and aggregators.

Maruti dropped the SHVS mild-hybrid system from the base LXi/VXi trims partway through this generation, which measurably lowered their certified figure vs the ZXi/ZXi+ below — this is a real running change, not a parse error.

CarDekho separately lists 22.1 km/kg for the same CNG variant. 25.51 is corroborated by Bajaj Finance, Spinny and 91Wheels-sourced news coverage, so used as the majority figure; flagging the CarDekho disagreement per the sourcing rule.

AMBIGUOUS — sources disagree materially on this figure. Bajaj Finance and CarDekho report 16 kmpl for the MT, while CarTrade, AckoDrive and Cars24 report 19.7-20.43 kmpl. This likely reflects Bajaj/CarDekho conflating an older pre-facelift K-series figure or a different trim. Used the majority-reported range pending manufacturer confirmation.
Same cross-source disagreement as the MT row above — Bajaj Finance's 20 kmpl for the automatic is the one figure that is NOT disputed, so confidence here is higher than for the MT row.

The kmpl and km/kg figures are numerically identical (17.44) for the petrol MT and iCNG MT respectively — this is a real coincidence confirmed across CarDekho, ZigWheels and Bajaj Finance, not a copy-paste error in this dataset.
Tata has not published an official DCA figure directly on its own site as of this pass; 18.50 kmpl is corroborated by Bajaj Finance and Spinny.
Cross-source disagreement: an earlier ARAI news cycle (cartoq, Sept 2024) reported 13.0 kmpl for this same MT petrol variant. Used Team-BHP's more recent brochure-sourced figure as primary.
Petrol MT and iVT (CVT) carry an identical ARAI-certified figure — confirmed by multiple independent outlets, not a data-entry duplicate.
iMT (clutch-less manual) normalized to transmission=mt per this dataset's rules — see README.

The Legender 4x4 NeoDrive mild-hybrid diesel AT variant shares this exact 14.4 kmpl figure (confirmed by CarTrade/CarDekho) — kept as one row rather than a separate 'hybrid' row since it's a 48V mild-hybrid, not a strong hybrid, consistent with how this dataset treats Maruti's SHVS (see README normalization rules).

CarDekho separately lists a much lower ~9 kmpl for this same variant — almost certainly a stale/incorrect figure (possibly referencing a pre-2016 BS4-era Crysta) rather than the current diesel-only GX. Autocar India and Team-BHP both independently confirm 15.6 kmpl, so used that. Crysta is diesel-manual-only in the current lineup — no petrol or automatic offered.

Older (2020-2021) Autocar India coverage of the original Sonet listed the turbo DCT at 18.3 kmpl and a turbo iMT at 18.2 kmpl; iMT figure not re-confirmed in the current variant-price list so omitted here rather than carrying forward a possibly-outdated number.
AMBIGUOUS — ZigWheels/CarDekho separately report 19.0 kmpl for this same diesel-AT variant instead of 18.6. Used Autocar India's current variant-price-list figure as primary.

Current on-sale generation is badged 'Carens Clavis' (facelift, revealed May 2025) — kept the model name as 'Carens' to match the requested list, since that's the nameplate buyers search for, but figures reflect the Clavis facelift specifically, not the original 2022 Carens.

An earlier ARAI disclosure cycle (cartoq, based on V3Cars data) reported 12.70 kmpl for this same MT variant instead of 12.17 — Mahindra was notably slow to publish Scorpio-N ARAI figures (over a year after launch), which may explain the two slightly different numbers circulating. Used the CarDekho/Bajaj Finance consensus figure.

cartoq/V3Cars report 15.42 kmpl for diesel MT instead of 15.94 — the 15.42 figure appears consistently for the diesel AT in every source, so it may be a copy-across error attributing the AT figure to the MT row in that one article. Used CarDekho/Bajaj Finance's 15.94 for MT.

CarDekho separately lists a much lower ~8 kmpl for petrol variants — likely stale data given Mahindra's pattern of not promptly publishing ARAI figures for this SUV (same issue seen with Scorpio-N). Used the carbike360/Bajaj Finance/ZigWheels consensus of 12.4 kmpl.
Bajaj Finance reports 10.2 kmpl for the same variant instead of 10.54 — minor (0.34 kmpl) disagreement, flagging for completeness.
Petrol MT and AT carry an identical ARAI-certified figure — confirmed consistently across CarWale, ZigWheels and Team-BHP, not a data-entry duplicate.
Diesel MT and AT also carry an identical figure — same situation as the petrol pair above; confirmed across multiple sources, not a duplicate entry error.

AckoDrive's '2022-2023' page lists a notably lower 18.72 kmpl for the same MT variant — likely an earlier pre-update certification, since CarDekho/91Wheels/ZigWheels all currently converge on 20.32 kmpl. Used the more recent, more consistent figure.

Figures reflect a recent update Autocar India describes as introducing a new 8-speed torque-converter automatic and improved mileage across the range (1.0 AT: 19.09 kmpl now vs 15.78 kmpl 'earlier'; 1.5 DSG: 18.72 kmpl vs 17.71 kmpl 'earlier'). The exact facelift date wasn't independently confirmed beyond Autocar's own June-2026 review — model_year_from=2025 is a reasonable-but-not-certain estimate pending a manufacturer-dated source.

AMBIGUOUS — ZigWheels/CarDekho report the OPPOSITE pairing (MT=13.79, CVT=12.34). Used Autocar India's figures as primary since it's the more current editorial source, but flagging the disagreement for manual verification.
